subtasks.description: Extract Microsoft licensed user counts and SKU details from SAM (Software Asset Management) system
Query consumption facts database to retrieve active user counts, actual spend, and usage metrics by SKU and date
Reconcile licensed users (SAM) against active users (consumption), calculate variance, identify under-provisioning vs over-provisioning by SKU, and generate recommendations

plan.cost_rationale: Three subtasks with dependencies. Extraction from SAM and consumption query can run in parallel (both simple sources). The reconciliation requires frontier tier because it involves multi-signal analysis (SAM license counts vs consumption facts), cross-source variance calculation, posture classification (over/under-provisioning), and actionable recommendations—all inherent to the cross_recon specialist with complex decision logic. The extract and sql_gen are straightforward single-source pulls: small for SAM extraction, mid for consumption SQL with aggregation by SKU and time.

output.task: Extract Microsoft licensed user counts and SKU details from SAM (Software Asset Management) system
output.query: Reconcile Microsoft licensing vs actual spend. Compare licensed users from SAM to active users from consumption facts and identify under/over-provisioning.
output.note: No document was provided to extract from. Please provide the SAM system report, export file, or document containing Microsoft licensed user counts and SKU details to proceed with extraction.

output.sql: SELECT cf.product, cf.event_date, SUM(cf.daily_active_seats) AS active_users, SUM(cf.amount_usd) AS actual_spend, COUNT(DISTINCT cf.user_id) AS distinct_users, AVG(cf.daily_active_seats) AS avg_daily_active_seats, SUM(cf.amount_usd) / NULLIF(SUM(cf.daily_active_seats), 0) AS cost_per_active_seat FROM consumption_facts cf WHERE cf.publisher = 'Microsoft' GROUP BY cf.product, cf.event_date ORDER BY cf.event_date DESC, cf.product

output.note: SAM extraction returned no data; unable to reconcile licensed users against consumption without primary source.
